name: CapForce Inc.
business: CapForce Inc. offers capital market listing sponsorship services to mid-sized growth-stage private companies, and is developing a next-generation global digital investment banking platform including cross-border securities trading, advanced computational model-enabled advisory, and fintech-enabled cap table management. The company's clients are primarily mid-sized, growth-stage private companies in Asia targeting public listings on exchanges such as NYSE, NASDAQ, HKEX, SGX, and TSE. CapForce also targets institutional investors and qualified high-net-worth individuals for its cross-border trading platform.
sellsTo: Asian issuers targeting U.S. and Asian exchanges
institutional and qualified individual investors for cross-border trading

exposures.sentence: CapForce is entering the financial technology industry supporting digital investment banking activities and capital table management.

Ongoing development initiatives include AI-driven analytics for identifying listing-related and liquidity risks, optimizing exchange selection, and supporting predictive insights related to post-listing performance.

catalysts.event: Acquisition of iCapX to provide cap table management platform
catalysts.sentence: in December 2025, the Company acquired all the issued and outstanding ordinary shares of Sun Investment Enterprises Limited, a company incorporated under the laws of the British Virgin Islands (the Holding Company ) from AEI Capital Ltd.

catalysts.event: Launch of next-generation global digital investment banking platform
catalysts.sentence: CapForce, following the acquisition of iCapX, is also progressing toward the launch of a next-generation global digital investment banking platform.

tamClaims.claim: Global financial advisory services market reached approximately $92.76 billion in 2025, projected to grow at CAGR of 6.1% to 6.72% through 2033, reaching $453.45 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Financial Advisory Services Market data as of December 2025, the global market for financial advisory services reached approximately $92.76 billion in 2025. It is now projected to grow at a compound annual rate (CAGR) of roughly 6.1% to 6.72% through 2033, reaching a projected value of approximately $453.45 billion when considering the broader financial consulting sector.

tamClaims.claim: Asia-Pacific accounted for approximately 43% of total IPO proceeds and hosted seven of the world's top 10 IPOs in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: For the full year ended December 31, 2025, the Asia-Pacific region accounted for approximately 43% of total proceeds and hosted seven of the world's top 10 IPOs, according to EY Global IPO Trends 2025.
